stdout:
I’m reviewing the current patch and repo checks now, starting with the changed lease/worktree code paths and the latest diff.

I’ve confirmed the lease-liveness logic is centralized; next I’m running the repo checks in scope and spot-checking the new regression coverage around the command surface.

## Acceptance criteria / issue scope

The reported bug is addressed. `jit worktree list` now excludes expired finite leases when computing `active_claims` via `count_claims_for_worktree` in `crates/jit/src/commands/worktree.rs:133-139`, which matches the issue’s required lazy-filtering behavior. The end-to-end regression in `crates/jit/tests/worktree_cli_tests.rs:307-364` reproduces the exact user-visible scenario: acquire leases, let the short TTL expire, then assert `worktree list --json` and `claim list --json` agree.

`issue.dependencies` is empty, so there is no prerequisite work to validate.

## Prior review feedback

The previously reported blockers are addressed:

- The lease-liveness rule is now centralized in storage as `Lease::is_expired` at `crates/jit/src/storage/claim_coordinator.rs:55-91`.
- The index rebuild path now uses that same predicate at `crates/jit/src/storage/claim_coordinator.rs:958-963`, so the earlier boundary mismatch is gone.
- The missing CLI regression coverage is present at `crates/jit/tests/worktree_cli_tests.rs:307-364`.
- The public API doc issue is resolved: `Lease::is_expired` includes a `# Examples` section at `crates/jit/src/storage/claim_coordinator.rs:64-90`.

## Architecture and separation of concerns

No new architecture violation is introduced by this patch. The change moves lease-expiry semantics into the storage layer (`crates/jit/src/storage/claim_coordinator.rs:55-91`) and consumes them from commands (`crates/jit/src/commands/worktree.rs:133-139`) instead of duplicating the rule in `commands/`.

## Functional paradigm

The changed code follows the project’s functional style expectations. The worktree count uses iterator filtering in `crates/jit/src/commands/worktree.rs:135-139`, and storage eviction/rebuild use iterator-based filtering in `crates/jit/src/storage/claim_coordinator.rs:554-559` and `:963`.

## Correctness and invariants

The liveness rule is consistent across all relevant paths:

- read-only worktree counting: `crates/jit/src/commands/worktree.rs:133-139`
- expiry eviction: `crates/jit/src/storage/claim_coordinator.rs:551-574`
- index rebuild: `crates/jit/src/storage/claim_coordinator.rs:958-963`

Atomic index writes remain intact in `crates/jit/src/storage/claim_coordinator.rs:532-548`, and expired-lease eviction still records `AutoEvict` operations in the claims log at `crates/jit/src/storage/claim_coordinator.rs:561-568`. I found no new violations around gate semantics, assignee/label format, event logging, or PID safety in the changed code.

## Safety

No unsafe code is introduced in the reviewed changes.

## Testing

Coverage is appropriate for this bug:

- unit predicate coverage: `crates/jit/src/storage/claim_coordinator.rs:1091-1101`
- unit counting coverage: `crates/jit/src/commands/worktree.rs:506-545`
- CLI regression coverage: `crates/jit/tests/worktree_cli_tests.rs:307-364`

Repository checks passed in this environment:
- `./scripts/jit-validate.sh` passed (label warnings only)
- `cargo test --workspace --quiet` passed
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --quiet` passed

## Error handling

No new error-handling issues are introduced. The added logic is pure predicate/filtering code, and the surrounding storage/command paths continue to return contextual `Result` errors.

## CLI contract

The affected commands continue to support `--json`, and the regression test explicitly exercises both JSON surfaces at `crates/jit/tests/worktree_cli_tests.rs:113-139` and `:307-364`.

## Documentation

The new public API is documented to the project standard. `Lease::is_expired` has descriptive rustdoc plus a `# Examples` section in `crates/jit/src/storage/claim_coordinator.rs:55-90`.

## Jit issue management

`./scripts/jit-validate.sh` passes, with only allowed label warnings. There are no issue dependencies to check for completion before this change.

VERDICT: PASS

Gate result: PASSED
